errmsg.description: Displays an error message to the operator. Supports %-style placeholders.

warning.description: Displays a warning message that does not stop data entry.

setvalue.description: Assigns a value to a variable or dictionary item.

getvalue.description: Returns the current value of a variable or dictionary item.

length.description: Returns the number of characters in a string.

concat.description: Concatenates two or more strings into one.

maketext.description: Builds a formatted string using %-style placeholders.

strip.description: Removes leading and trailing spaces from a string.

tonumber.description: Converts a string to a numeric value.

pos.description: Returns the position of a substring within a string (0 if not found).

skip.description: Skips to a specified field, bypassing intermediate fields.

recode.description: Maps ranges of input values to output values.

count.description: Counts the number of occurrences that satisfy a condition.

seek.description: Searches occurrences of a group for a matching condition.

sum.description: Returns the sum of an item across occurrences.

min.description: Returns the minimum value among the arguments.

max.description: Returns the maximum value among the arguments.

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of a number.

int.description: Returns the integer portion of a number.

round.description: Rounds a number to the nearest integer or given decimals.

random.description: Returns a random number within a range.

visualvalue.description: Returns the value currently displayed for a field.

reenter.description: Forces the operator to re-enter the current or a specified field.

accept.description: Displays a menu of choices and returns the selected number.

prompt.description: Prompts the operator for free-text input.

impute.description: Assigns a value to an item as part of imputation/editing.

writecase.description: Writes the current case to the output data file.

loadcase.description: Loads a case from a dictionary/data source by key.

now.description: Returns the current system date and time.

datediff.description: Returns the difference between two dates in a given unit.

fileexists.description: Returns 1 if the given file exists, otherwise 0.
